title: Istanbul/Sile

sections.heading: __intro__
sections.text:
Şile is a beach resort, part of the metropolis of Istanbul. It’s 75 km northeast of the big city on the Black Sea coast, so its sandy beach is a big draw in summer.

Ağva is a smaller quieter resort on the coast 30 km further east, also within the municipality of Şile. That also includes the hills further south, which have camping and hiking areas around reservoir lakes.

sections.heading: Understand
sections.text:
Taking its name from the Turkish word for marjoram, Şile has evidence of settlement from the Stone Age, and in the Hellenistic period it grew into the port-city of Hilea. It had limited strategic value and was outgrown by Constantinople, falling into that gravitational pull in the Roman / Byzantine era and nowadays forming a district of the sprawling metropolis of Istanbul.

Şile has one of the few decent beaches within a day-trip from Istanbul – much of the Black Sea coast is rocky, the Marmara coast is industrial, and the Aegean resorts are too far. So it’s encrusted by city dwellers’ holiday homes, and busy on fine weekends. Few international tourists come this way.

sections.heading: Get in
sections.text:
See Istanbul#Get in for long-distance transport. By road from the city or SAW airport follow Highway D-020; from IST airport use O-7 (toll) to bypass city centre. 

IETT Bus 139 runs every two hours from Üsküdar, taking two hours via Ümraniye and Sultanbeyli. Bus 139A continues to Ağva.

 is on Bayrak Cd, 500 m south of Old Town.

sections.heading: Get around
sections.text:
Buses and dolmuşes ply along the main road between Şile and Ağva. You need your own wheels to get up into the hills. 

Şile Taxi is on +90 216 711 2127 and there's a taxi rank by the bus station.

sections.heading: See
sections.text:
* is strung along Üsküdar Cd, pedestrian-only in the evening. The modern shop fronts don't do much for it. 
* Hüseyin Keçici Central Mosque is midway along Üsküdar Cd. The interior is richly decorated. 
* 
* 
* meaning "wailing rocks" is a headland and beach. 
* was created in 1988 for water supply. It's above the village of Erenler, on the old road struggling over the hills from Istanbul to Şile. As of 2026, entry is prohibited to the dam and its surrounds. South edge of the village on Dersaadet Sk is a fine Ottoman tomb, but nobody knows who or when he was.
* meaning "hidden lake" is a small reservoir lake 8 km south of town. The road up to it is dotted with eating places.
* is a former Greek village with multiple ruins. The "waterfall" south edge of it is little over a metre high, but there are forest strolls nearby.
* is a small beach resort 28 km east of Şile.

sections.heading: Do
sections.text:
* Şile Culture Centre is at Atatürk Cd 30.
* is a 600 m strip of sand east of "Wailing Rocks". In 2026 you pay 300 TL for parking and 900 TL to get on the beach. It ends at a small tombola islet then the much quieter Kırandere beach continues east for another 1 km.
* Ayazma beach stretches for 1.5 km west of the harbour. It's the closest to town centre, sandy but overcrowded and grubby.
* west of Ayazma is quieter and more relaxing. This strip continues as Vadi, Kırandere and Alacalı beaches.
* 
* 

* 
* is a camping and backwoods area by Omerli barrage lake, 1 km north of Esenceli village. The access road is in poor condition, and a morass after rain. 
* is a small village, the start of hiking trails into the wooded hills.

sections.heading: Buy
sections.text:
The shopping strip is along Üsküdar Cd and Cumhuriyet Cd. The town has a distinctive see-though "Şile Cloth".

The Bazaar and farmers market is at Pazar Sk off Cumhuriyet Cd, trading F 08:00-18:00.

sections.heading: Eat
sections.text:
* Street food: handcarts sell kokoreç, corn and simit / poğaça. 
* Şile pier looking onto the castle has eating places including Irmera Cafe, Rıhtım Çay Bahçesi, [http://www.artanebalik.com/ Artane Balık], Kılçık Balık, Ali Baba, Ebru Balık and İskorpit. Several more on the shore road Liman Sk.
* 
* [https://www.amarasile.com/ Amara Şile] also gets good reviews. It's on the headland 100 m east of Şile İyot, open daily 09:00-00:00. 
* 
* 
* 
* 
* 
*

sections.heading: Sleep
sections.text:
* 
* 
* 
* [https://miletusotel.com/ Miletus Otel ] is by Sign Değirmen Hotel at Ayazma Cd 1. 
* 
*

sections.heading: Connect
sections.text: Şile and its approach roads have 4G from all Turkish carriers. As of April 2026, 5G has not reached this area.

sections.heading: Go next
sections.text:
* Public transport means heading back to Istanbul. Take the ferry from Üsküdar across the Bosphorus to the Old City of Sultanahmet.
* With your own vehicle, wind along the Black Sea coast.
* Or take motorway O-7 south to historic Bursa.

[Understand]
Taking its name from the Turkish word for marjoram, Şile has evidence of settlement from the Stone Age, and in the Hellenistic period it grew into the port-city of Hilea. It had limited strategic value and was outgrown by Constantinople, falling into that gravitational pull in the Roman / Byzantine era and nowadays forming a district of the sprawling metropolis of Istanbul.

Şile has one of the few decent beaches within a day-trip from Istanbul – much of the Black Sea coast is rocky, the Marmara coast is industrial, and the Aegean resorts are too far.
